title: Getting Started with lyricsLab
text: lyricsLab R package analyzing song lyrics Spotify listening history. guide help get started just minutes.

title: Getting Started with lyricsLab
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("Coxabc/lyricsLab")

what: 1. Get Genius API Token (for lyrics analysis)
title: Getting Started with lyricsLab
text: Step 1: Go https://genius.com/api-clientsStep 2: Sign log (free)Step 3: Click “New API Client” fill : App Name: lyricsLab App Website: http://localhost Redirect URI: (leave default) Step 4: Copy “Client Access Token”Step 5: Set R:
code: Sys.setenv(GENIUS_API_TOKEN = "your_token_here")

what: 2. Get Spotify API Credentials (for listening history)
title: Getting Started with lyricsLab
text: Step 1: Go https://developer.spotify.com/dashboardStep 2: Log Spotify accountStep 3: Click “Create app” fill : App name: lyricsLab App description: R package music analysis Redirect URI: http://127.0.0.1:1410/ Step 4: Copy Client ID Client SecretStep 5: Set R: Note: Spotify’s Web API requires Spotify Premium account.
code: Sys.setenv(SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID     = "your_client_id") Sys.setenv(SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET = "your_client_secret")

title: Getting Started with lyricsLab
code: library(lyricsLab)  # 1. Authenticate with Spotify spotify_auth()  # 2. Get lyrics for a single song lyrics <- get_lyrics("Ed Sheeran", "Drive")  # 3. Run analysis sentiment_score(lyrics) complexity_score(lyrics) analyze_emotions(lyrics) theme_detection(lyrics)  # 4. Plot plot_sentiment_arc(lyrics) plot_emotions_web(lyrics) plot_themes(lyrics)

title: Getting Started with lyricsLab
code: spotify_auth()  analysis <- analyze_playlist(   "https://open.spotify.com/playlist/37i9dQZF1DXcBWIGoYBM5M",   max_songs = 50 )  plot_sentiment(analysis) plot_complexity(analysis) plot_scatter(analysis) plot_top_complex(analysis)  calculate_listening_diversity(analysis)

title: Getting Started with lyricsLab
code: spotify_auth() history <- get_recently_played()  plot_listening_patterns(history, type = "hourly") plot_listening_patterns(history, type = "daily") plot_listening_patterns(history, type = "weekly")  calculate_listening_diversity(history)

title: Analyzing a Single Song
text: Set Genius API token starting. can get one https://genius.com/api-clients.
code: Sys.setenv(GENIUS_API_TOKEN = "your_token_here") library(lyricsLab)

title: Analyzing a Single Song
text: get_lyrics() searches Genius song, scrapes lyrics page, returns lyrics_lab object containing lyrics, metadata, detected song structure. Use preview_lyrics() check retrieved: Use view_lyrics() print everything:
code: lyrics <- get_lyrics("Billie Eilish", "bad guy") preview_lyrics(lyrics) #> Song:     bad guy #> Artist:   Billie Eilish #> Album:    WHEN WE ALL FALL ASLEEP, WHERE DO WE GO? #> Lines:    47 #> URL:      https://genius.com/... #> Sections: verse, chorus, outro #> #> duh #> I'm the bad guy #> duh #> I'm the bad guy #> ... view_lyrics(lyrics)

title: Analyzing a Single Song
text: sentiment_score() computes single score whole song using AFINN lexicon (bing/nrc prefer). prints summary returns results invisibly. Switch methods method argument:
code: result <- sentiment_score(lyrics) #> #> === Sentiment: bad guy by Billie Eilish === #>   Method:          afinn #>   Score:           -0.34 #>   Normalized:      46.6 / 100 #>   Interpretation:  Neutral - balanced emotional tone #>   Positive lines:  19.1% #>   Negative lines:  25.5% sentiment_score(lyrics, method = "bing") sentiment_score(lyrics, method = "nrc")

title: Analyzing a Single Song
text: sentiment_arc() scores every line individually describes sentiment shifts song: Visualize arc plot_sentiment_arc(): point one lyric line, coloured green (positive) red (negative). smoothing line shows overall trajectory. Turn smooth = FALSE:
code: arc <- sentiment_arc(lyrics) #> #> === Sentiment Arc: bad guy by Billie Eilish === #>   Method:          afinn #>   Mean sentiment:  -0.34 #>   Trajectory:      Maintains consistent tone #>   Positive lines:  9 #>   Negative lines:  12 #>   Neutral lines:   26 plot_sentiment_arc(lyrics) plot_sentiment_arc(lyrics, smooth = FALSE)

title: Analyzing a Single Song
code: positive_negative_ratio(lyrics) #> #> === Positive/Negative Ratio: bad guy by Billie Eilish === #>   Ratio:           0.75:1 (positive:negative) #>   Interpretation:  Balanced #>   Positive lines:  9  (19.1%) #>   Negative lines:  12 (25.5%) #>   Neutral lines:   26 (55.3%)

title: Analyzing a Single Song
text: analyze_emotions() uses NRC lexicon detect eight emotions: anger, anticipation, disgust, fear, joy, sadness, surprise, trust. Two plot options — bar chart radar/web chart: radar chart useful comparing emotional “shape” songs side side visually.
code: analyze_emotions(lyrics) #> #> === Emotions: bad guy by Billie Eilish === #>   anticipation   3 words (0.51%) #>   joy            2 words (0.34%) #>   trust          2 words (0.34%) #>   anger          1 words (0.17%) #>   ... #>   Dominant emotion: anticipation plot_emotions_bar(lyrics) plot_emotions_web(lyrics)

title: Analyzing a Single Song
text: complexity_score() measures vocabulary richness, average word length, syllables per word, estimates Flesch-Kincaid reading level, combining single 0–100 complexity score. high type-token ratio (unique/total) indicates varied vocabulary. Reading level estimated grade level — elementary graduate.
code: complexity_score(lyrics) #> #> === Complexity: bad guy by Billie Eilish === #>   Vocabulary size:     106 unique / 210 total words #>   Vocabulary richness: 0.505 #>   Avg word length:     3.81 characters #>   Syllables per word:  1.29 #>   Reading level:       3.2 (grade) #>   Complexity score:    24.6 / 100 #>   Interpretation:      Elementary level - very simple vocabulary

title: Analyzing a Single Song
text: theme_detection() matches lyrics keyword lists across 23 theme categories shows top matches: Plot theme breakdown:
code: theme_detection(lyrics) #> #> === Themes: bad guy by Billie Eilish === #>   Lust/Desire            4 matches (22.2%) #>   Jealousy               3 matches (16.7%) #>   Rebellion              3 matches (16.7%) #>   Empowerment            3 matches (16.7%) #>   Time/Change            2 matches (11.1%) #> #>   Dominant theme: Lust/Desire plot_themes(lyrics)

title: Analyzing a Single Song
text: analysis functions print console return results invisibly. Capture assigning variable:
code: sent   <- sentiment_score(lyrics) comp   <- complexity_score(lyrics) themes <- theme_detection(lyrics) emo    <- analyze_emotions(lyrics)

title: Spotify Integration: Playlists and Listening History
text: need Spotify app start. Go https://developer.spotify.com/dashboard, create app, set redirect URI exactly http://127.0.0.1:1410/. Store credentials environment — easiest way add ~/.Renviron load automatically every session: authenticate: opens browser window. Log , authorize app, ’ll redirected back automatically. token saved global environment session. expires hour — just run spotify_auth() hit auth errors. scopes requested : recently played, top tracks, saved library, private playlists.
code: SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID=your_client_id SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET=your_client_secret GENIUS_API_TOKEN=your_genius_token library(lyricsLab) spotify_auth()

title: Spotify Integration: Playlists and Listening History
text: get_recently_played() returns 50 recent plays (maximum Spotify allows per request): returned data frame columns: played_at, track_name, track_id, artist_name, artist_id, album_name, album_id, duration_ms, popularity. Quick summaries directly data frame:
code: history <- get_recently_played() #> #> === Recently Played === #>   Tracks retrieved:  50 #>   From:              2024-01-10 08:14 #>   To:                2024-01-12 23:47 #>   Time span:         63.5 hours # Most played artists sort(table(history$artist_name), decreasing = TRUE)  # Most played tracks sort(table(history$track_name), decreasing = TRUE)  # Average popularity of what you've been listening to mean(history$popularity, na.rm = TRUE)

title: Spotify Integration: Playlists and Listening History
text: plot_listening_patterns() takes history data frame type argument:
code: # By hour of day plot_listening_patterns(history, type = "hourly")  # By date (trend over time) plot_listening_patterns(history, type = "daily")  # By day of week plot_listening_patterns(history, type = "weekly")

title: Spotify Integration: Playlists and Listening History
text: calculate_listening_diversity() measures spread listening across artists using Shannon entropy, producing 0–100 diversity score: score 80 means explore broadly; 40 means mostly stick handful artists.
code: calculate_listening_diversity(history) #> #> === Listening Diversity === #>   Unique artists:    23 #>   Unique tracks:     47 #>   Total plays:       50 #>   Top artist:        Taylor Swift (18.0% of plays) #>   Diversity score:   71.4 / 100 #>   Interpretation:    Moderately diverse - good mix of variety and favorites

title: Spotify Integration: Playlists and Listening History
text: get_top_tracks() pulls Spotify top tracks. time_range argument controls window:
code: # Last 4 weeks recent_tops <- get_top_tracks(time_range = "short_term",  limit = 20)  # Last 6 months (default) medium_tops <- get_top_tracks(time_range = "medium_term", limit = 20)  # All time all_time    <- get_top_tracks(time_range = "long_term",   limit = 50)  # View ranked list all_time[, c("rank", "track_name", "artist_name", "popularity")]

title: Spotify Integration: Playlists and Listening History
text: get_playlist_tracks() accepts full Spotify URL just playlist ID: works playlists size. returned data frame includes track_name, artist_name, album_name, popularity, duration_ms, added_at, added_by.
code: playlist <- get_playlist_tracks(   "https://open.spotify.com/playlist/37i9dQZF1DXcBWIGoYBM5M" )  # Or just the ID playlist <- get_playlist_tracks("37i9dQZF1DXcBWIGoYBM5M") # Most popular tracks in the playlist playlist[order(-playlist$popularity), c("track_name", "artist_name", "popularity")]  # Artists with the most tracks sort(table(playlist$artist_name), decreasing = TRUE)

title: Spotify Integration: Playlists and Listening History
text: analyze_playlist() combines playlist fetching Genius lyrics retrieval full analysis every song. calls get_lyrics(), sentiment_score(), complexity_score(), theme_detection() track, rate limiting requests avoid hitting API limits. Progress printed song processed: Songs can’t found Genius (instrumentals, new releases, non-English tracks) skipped listed end.
code: analysis <- analyze_playlist(   "https://open.spotify.com/playlist/37i9dQZF1DXcBWIGoYBM5M",   max_songs = 20,   # limit for speed, default 50   delay     = 2     # seconds between requests ) Analyzing lyrics for 20 songs...  [1/20] Anti-Hero by Taylor Swift   + Sentiment: -0.21 | Complexity: 28.4/100 | Theme: Self-Reflection [2/20] Flowers by Miley Cyrus   + Sentiment: 0.84 | Complexity: 31.2/100 | Theme: Empowerment ...  === PLAYLIST ANALYSIS COMPLETE === Playlist: Today's Top Hits Analyzed: 17/20 tracks Avg sentiment: 0.12 Avg complexity: 29.8/100 Most common theme: Love/Romance

title: Spotify Integration: Playlists and Listening History
text: plot function takes analysis result directly:
code: # Distribution of sentiment scores across the playlist plot_sentiment(analysis)  # Distribution of complexity scores plot_complexity(analysis)  # Which themes appear most across the playlist plot_themes(analysis)  # Is there a relationship between complexity and sentiment? plot_scatter(analysis)  # The 10 most lyrically complex songs plot_top_complex(analysis)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Alyssa Cox. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Cox (2026). lyricsLab: Package (One Line, Title Case). R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://coxabc.github.io/lyricsLab/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {lyricsLab: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)},   author = {Alyssa Cox},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://coxabc.github.io/lyricsLab/}, }

what: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: R package fetching, analyzing, visualizing song lyrics. Connect Spotify account pull playlists listening history, retrieve lyrics Genius, run sentiment, complexity, emotion, theme analysis.

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("Coxabc/lyricsLab")

what: 1. Get Genius API Token (for lyrics analysis)
title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: Step 1: Go https://genius.com/api-clientsStep 2: Sign log (free)Step 3: Click “New API Client” fill : - App Name: lyricsLab - App Website: http://localhost - Redirect URI: (leave default) Step 4: Copy “Client Access Token”Step 5: Set R:
code: # For current session only Sys.setenv(GENIUS_API_TOKEN = "your_token_here")

what: 2. Get Spotify API Credentials (for listening history)

text: Step 1: Go https://developer.spotify.com/dashboardStep 2: Log Spotify accountStep 3: Click “Create app” fill : - App name: lyricsLab - App description: R package music analysis - Redirect URI: http://127.0.0.1:1410/ Step 4: Copy Client ID Client SecretStep 5: Set R:
code: Sys.setenv(SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID= "your_client_id") Sys.setenv(SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET= "your_client_secret")

Run analysis sentiment_score(lyrics) complexity_score(lyrics) analyze_emotions(lyrics) theme_detection(lyrics)  # 4. Plot plot_sentiment_arc(lyrics) plot_emotions_web(lyrics) plot_themes(lyrics)

what: Analyze Emotions Using NRC Lexicon — analyze_emotions
title: Analyze Emotions Using NRC Lexicon — analyze_emotions
text: Detects presence 8 emotions: joy, sadness, anger, fear, anticipation, trust, surprise, disgust. Prints clean summary console returns results invisibly.

title: Analyze Emotions Using NRC Lexicon — analyze_emotions
code: analyze_emotions(lyrics_obj, normalize = TRUE)

title: Analyze Emotions Using NRC Lexicon — analyze_emotions
text: lyrics_obj Lyrics object get_lyrics() normalize Logical; normalize counts total words? Default TRUE

title: Analyze Emotions Using NRC Lexicon — analyze_emotions
text: Invisibly returns list containing: emotions - Data frame emotion counts percentages dominant_emotion - Name top emotion summary - One-line text summary

title: Analyze Emotions Using NRC Lexicon — analyze_emotions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lyrics <- get_lyrics("Billie Eilish", "bad guy") analyze_emotions(lyrics)  # Store result invisibly if needed result <- analyze_emotions(lyrics) } # }

code: analyze_playlist(playlist_url, max_songs = 50, delay = 2, token = NULL)

title: Analyze Playlist Lyrics — analyze_playlist
text: playlist_url Spotify playlist URL ID max_songs Maximum number songs analyze (default 50) delay Delay API calls seconds (default 2) token Spotify token (uses saved token NULL)

title: Analyze Playlist Lyrics — analyze_playlist
text: List playlist tracks lyrics analysis

title: Analyze Playlist Lyrics — analyze_playlist
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ analysis <- analyze_playlist(   "https://open.spotify.com/playlist/37i9dQZF1DXcBWIGoYBM5M",   max_songs = 20 ) print(analysis) plot_sentiment(analysis) } # }

title: Calculate Listening Diversity — calculate_listening_diversity
text: Measures diverse listening habits using Shannon entropy. Prints clean summary console returns results invisibly.

title: Calculate Listening Diversity — calculate_listening_diversity
code: calculate_listening_diversity(history_df)

title: Calculate Listening Diversity — calculate_listening_diversity
text: history_df Data frame get_recently_played()

title: Calculate Listening Diversity — calculate_listening_diversity
text: Invisibly returns list containing: unique_artists - Number unique artists unique_tracks - Number unique tracks total_plays - Total number plays top_artist - played artist top_artist_percentage - Percentage plays top artist diversity_score - Score 0-100 (higher = diverse) interpretation - Text description diversity

title: Calculate Listening Diversity — calculate_listening_diversity
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ history <- get_recently_played(n_total = 500) calculate_listening_diversity(history)  playlist <- get_playlist_tracks("https://open.spotify.com/playlist/123abc") calculate_listening_diversity(playlist) } # }

title: Calculate Lyrical Complexity — complexity_score
text: Computes comprehensive complexity metrics including vocabulary richness, average word length, reading level, syllable complexity. Prints clean summary console returns results invisibly.

title: Calculate Lyrical Complexity — complexity_score
code: complexity_score(lyrics_obj)

title: Calculate Lyrical Complexity — complexity_score
text: lyrics_obj Lyrics object get_lyrics()

title: Calculate Lyrical Complexity — complexity_score
text: Invisibly returns list containing: vocabulary_size - Number unique words total_words - Total word count type_token_ratio - Vocabulary richness (0-1) avg_word_length - Mean characters per word reading_level - Estimated grade level (Flesch-Kincaid) syllables_per_word - Average syllables per word interpretation - Text description complexity complexity_score - Overall score (0-100) song - Track title artist - Artist name

title: Calculate Lyrical Complexity — complexity_score
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lyrics <- get_lyrics("Kendrick Lamar", "HUMBLE.") complexity_score(lyrics)  # Store result invisibly if needed result <- complexity_score(lyrics) } # }

what: Get Lyrics from Genius (Direct API + Simple Scraping) — get_lyrics
title: Get Lyrics from Genius (Direct API + Simple Scraping) — get_lyrics
text: Retrieves song lyrics directly calling Genius API scraping lyrics Genius website using rvest's html_text2().

title: Get Lyrics from Genius (Direct API + Simple Scraping) — get_lyrics
code: get_lyrics(artist, song, access_token = NULL)

title: Get Lyrics from Genius (Direct API + Simple Scraping) — get_lyrics
text: artist Character string artist name song Character string song title access_token Genius API token. NULL, uses GENIUS_API_TOKEN env var

title: Get Lyrics from Genius (Direct API + Simple Scraping) — get_lyrics
text: list class "lyrics_lab" containing: lyrics - Character vector lyrics (one element per line) metadata - List song info structure - Data frame detected sections

title: Get Lyrics from Genius (Direct API + Simple Scraping) — get_lyrics
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Set your token first Sys.setenv(GENIUS_API_TOKEN = "your_token_here")  # Get lyrics lyrics <- get_lyrics(artist = "Taylor Swift", song = "Anti-Hero") } # }

title: Calculate Positive/Negative Ratio — positive_negative_ratio
text: Computes balance positive negative sentiment lines. Prints clean summary console returns results invisibly.

title: Calculate Positive/Negative Ratio — positive_negative_ratio
code: positive_negative_ratio(lyrics_obj)

title: Calculate Positive/Negative Ratio — positive_negative_ratio
text: lyrics_obj Lyrics object get_lyrics()

title: Calculate Positive/Negative Ratio — positive_negative_ratio
text: Invisibly returns list containing: ratio - Positive negative ratio (Inf negative lines) ratio_text - Human-readable ratio string interpretation - Text description balance positive_lines - Count positive lines negative_lines - Count negative lines neutral_lines - Count neutral lines positive_percentage - Percentage positive lines negative_percentage - Percentage negative lines neutral_percentage - Percentage neutral lines

title: Calculate Positive/Negative Ratio — positive_negative_ratio
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lyrics <- get_lyrics("Pharrell Williams", "Happy") positive_negative_ratio(lyrics) } # }

text: x lyrics_lab object n Number lines show. Default 5

title: Calculate Sentiment Arc — sentiment_arc
text: Calculates sentiment scores line lyrics prints clean summary console. Use plot_sentiment_arc() visualize.

title: Calculate Sentiment Arc — sentiment_arc
code: sentiment_arc(lyrics_obj, method = "afinn")

title: Calculate Sentiment Arc — sentiment_arc
text: lyrics_obj Lyrics object get_lyrics() method Sentiment method: "afinn", "bing", "nrc". Default "afinn"

title: Calculate Sentiment Arc — sentiment_arc
text: Invisibly returns list containing: scores - Data frame line--line sentiment scores mean_sentiment - Mean sentiment across lines median_sentiment - Median sentiment range - Min max sentiment scores sd - Standard deviation trajectory - sentiment changes song positive_lines - Count positive lines negative_lines - Count negative lines neutral_lines - Count neutral lines song - Track title artist - Artist name method - Method used

title: Calculate Sentiment Arc — sentiment_arc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lyrics <- get_lyrics("Taylor Swift", "Anti-Hero") sentiment_arc(lyrics)  # Store result invisibly if needed result <- sentiment_arc(lyrics) } # }

title: Calculate Overall Sentiment Score — sentiment_score
text: Computes single sentiment score entire lyrics using syuzhet package. Prints clean summary console returns results invisibly.

title: Calculate Overall Sentiment Score — sentiment_score
code: sentiment_score(lyrics_obj, method = "afinn")

title: Calculate Overall Sentiment Score — sentiment_score
text: lyrics_obj Lyrics object get_lyrics() method Sentiment method: "afinn", "bing", "nrc". Default "afinn"

title: Calculate Overall Sentiment Score — sentiment_score
text: Invisibly returns list containing: score - Raw sentiment score normalized_score - Score normalized 0-100 interpretation - Text description sentiment method - Method used song - Track title artist - Artist name positive_ratio - Proportion positive lines negative_ratio - Proportion negative lines scores - Per-line sentiment scores

title: Calculate Overall Sentiment Score — sentiment_score
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lyrics <- get_lyrics("Adele", "Someone Like You") sentiment_score(lyrics)  # Use a different method sentiment_score(lyrics, method = "bing") } # }
